examples.title: Process Creation Pseudocode
examples.description: Demonstrates how a process is created and PCB is initialized
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows how an OS creates a new process by allocating and initializing a Process Control Block, loading the program into memory, and placing the process in the ready queue.

examples.title: Context Switching Simulation in Python
examples.description: Demonstrates the concept of context switching between processes
examples.code:
class Process:
    def __init__(self, pid, name, burst_time):
        self.pid = pid
        self.name = name
        self.burst_time = burst_time
        self.remaining_time = burst_time
        self.state = "NEW"
        
class ContextSwitchSimulator:
    def __init__(self):
        self.ready_queue = []
        self.current_process = None
        
    def add_process(self, process):
        process.state = "READY"
        self.ready_queue.append(process)
        
    def save_context(self, process):
        # Save current process state to its PCB
        process.cpu_registers = self.get_registers()
        process.program_counter = self.get_pc()
        print(f"Saving context for Process {process.pid}")
        
    def load_context(self, process):
        # Restore process state from its PCB
        self.set_registers(process.cpu_registers)
        self.set_pc(process.program_counter)
        process.state = "RUNNING"
        print(f"Loaded context for Process {process.pid}")
        
    def schedule(self):
        if self.current_process:
            self.save_context(self.current_process)
            self.current_process.state = "READY"
            self.ready_queue.append(self.current_process)
            
        if self.ready_queue:
            self.current_process = self.ready_queue.pop(0)
            self.load_context(self.current_process)

# Example usage
sim = ContextSwitchSimulator()
p1 = Process(1, "P1", 10)
p2 = Process(2, "P2", 5)
sim.add_process(p1)
sim.add_process(p2)
sim.schedule()
examples.explanation: This Python code simulates context switching by saving and loading process states. The save_context method stores CPU registers and program counter to the process's PCB, while load_context restores them when a new process gets the CPU.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) where n is number of processes

examples.title: Process Creation in C (fork)
examples.description: Demonstrates creating a child process using fork() system call
examples.explanation: This C program demonstrates process creation using the fork() system call. fork() creates an exact copy of the parent process (child). The return value differs: 0 in child, child's PID in parent, -1 on error. The parent uses wait() to synchronize with child termination.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for fork, O(n) for execution

examples.title: Simple Round Robin Scheduler
examples.description: Demonstrates a basic round-robin CPU scheduling algorithm
examples.code:
ALGORITHM RoundRobinScheduler
INPUT: Time quantum Q
VARIABLES: ReadyQueue, CurrentTime, CurrentProcess

BEGIN
  Initialize ReadyQueue as empty
  CurrentTime ← 0
  
  // Add all new processes to ready queue
  FOR each new process P DO
    P.state ← READY
    Enqueue(ReadyQueue, P)
  END FOR
  
  WHILE ReadyQueue is not empty OR processes remaining DO
    // Get next process from ready queue
    CurrentProcess ← Dequeue(ReadyQueue)
    CurrentProcess.state ← RUNNING
    
    // Execute for quantum or until blocked
    IF CurrentProcess.remainingTime > Q THEN
      CurrentTime ← CurrentTime + Q
      CurrentProcess.remainingTime ← CurrentProcess.remainingTime - Q
      
      // Time quantum expired - preempt and requeue
      CurrentProcess.state ← READY
      Enqueue(ReadyQueue, CurrentProcess)
      Print "Process", CurrentProcess.pid, "preempted at time", CurrentTime
      
    ELSE
      // Process completes within quantum
      CurrentTime ← CurrentTime + CurrentProcess.remainingTime
      CurrentProcess.remainingTime ← 0
      CurrentProcess.state ← TERMINATED
      Print "Process", CurrentProcess.pid, "completed at time", CurrentTime
    END IF
    
  END WHILE
  
  Print "All processes completed. Total time:", CurrentTime
END
examples.explanation: Round Robin scheduling gives each process an equal time slice (quantum). If a process doesn't complete within its quantum, it's preempted and moved to the back of the ready queue. This ensures fair CPU distribution and is commonly used in time-sharing systems.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n = number of processes
examples.spaceComplexity: O(m) where m = capacity of ready queue
